proposed_patient_facing_text: If a possible infection is making someone feel very unwell or rapidly worse, they should get urgent clinical assessment. HomeLab cannot confirm sepsis; severe or fast-worsening symptoms should be treated as urgent.
intended_gap_filled: Strengthens severe infection escalation and clinical-assessment limitation wording.
evidence_excerpt_or_source_note: Source note: NICE guideline covers recognition, early assessment, escalating care, and support for suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over.
duplicate_check_note: Not present as a v1_2 KB source; complements existing nice_sepsis_overview items without using lab-operations sources.
safety_note: High-risk red-flag wording; must remain conservative, non-diagnostic, and manually reviewed.

proposed_patient_facing_text: Chest pain with sweating, feeling sick, light-headedness, or shortness of breath needs immediate medical help. HomeLab cannot decide whether chest pain is a heart attack; seek emergency care when these warning signs are present.
intended_gap_filled: Strengthens emergency symptom-cluster retrieval and urgent-care phrasing.
evidence_excerpt_or_source_note: Source note: NHS chest pain page says to call emergency help for chest pain with sweating, sickness, light-headedness, or shortness of breath.
duplicate_check_note: Same source already represented in v1_2; candidate is a focused wording refinement, not a new domain or duplicate source.
safety_note: Do not diagnose heart attack; keep emergency escalation grounded and review-required.

proposed_patient_facing_text: Shortness of breath can be serious if lips or skin look very pale, blue, or grey, or if the person becomes suddenly confused. HomeLab cannot diagnose the cause; these warning signs need emergency help.
intended_gap_filled: Strengthens severe breathlessness emergency phrasing and limitation wording.
evidence_excerpt_or_source_note: Source note: NHS shortness of breath page lists pale, blue, or grey lips/skin and sudden confusion as emergency warning signs.
duplicate_check_note: Same source already represented in v1_2; candidate improves retrieval for severe breathlessness warning signs.
safety_note: Do not diagnose the cause of breathlessness; keep emergency wording source-grounded and manually reviewed.

proposed_patient_facing_text: Blood tests can help check health, investigate symptoms, or monitor a condition, but results can be complicated and may not give the full answer on their own. Ask a healthcare professional to explain what the result means and whether more tests are needed.
intended_gap_filled: Adds concise limitation/disclaimer wording for blood-test interpretation.
evidence_excerpt_or_source_note: Source note: NHS blood tests page says tests may check health or symptoms, results can be complicated, and other tests may sometimes be needed.
duplicate_check_note: Uses existing blood_tests source; avoids nhs_blood_tests exact duplicate and does not rely on held-out MedlinePlus overview.
safety_note: Patient-facing explainer only; do not imply that test results alone diagnose or rule out conditions.
